title: lichen_l2_iface_init has no error propagation
description:
lichen_l2_iface_init() is a void function that cannot report initialization failures to its caller (NET_DEVICE_INIT). Errors are logged but the interface appears initialized even on failure.

Location:
- lichen_l2.c:326-384 (lichen_l2_iface_init)

What goes wrong: If lichen_lora_l2_init() fails (line 334) or lichen_lora_l2_get_eui64() returns NULL (line 341), the function returns early but:
- lichen_iface may be left NULL or partially set
- link_ctx_initialized stays 0
- RX callback is not registered
- The net_if appears valid to the IPv6 stack

Subsequent calls to lichen_l2_send() or lichen_l2_enable() will encounter undefined states. The IPv6 stack may try to send packets to an interface that silently drops them.

Fix: Zephyr's net_if_api.init is void, so error reporting is limited. Options:
1. Set an internal error flag checked by enable/send
2. Fail more loudly (LOG_ERR + panic in debug builds)
3. Document that lichen_lora_l2_is_running() must be checked before use
close_reason: Duplicate of .2 - same init error propagation issue
